folio.label: 147r
folio.l: Lo how I vanysshe , flessú blood and skyn
Allas whan shuln myne bones , ben at reste
Moder witú ©ow , wolde I chaunge my cheste
That in my chaumbre , longe tyme hatú be
ña for an heire clout , to wrappe Inne me
But ©et to me , she wol nat do that grace
For whicú ful pale , and welked is my face
But sires to ©ow , it is no curteisie
To speken to an olde man , veleynye
But he trespace in word , or elles in dede
In holy wryt , ©e moun ©oure self wel rede
A geyns an olde man , hoor vp on his hed
ñe shulde a ryse , wherfore I ©eue ©ow red
Ne dotú vn to an old man , noon harm now
Nomore than that ©e wolde , a man dide ©ow
In age , if that ©e so longe a byde
And god be witú ©ow , where ©e go or ryde
I mot go thider , as I haue to goo
Nay olde cherl by god , thow shalt nat so
Seide this other hasardour , a noon
Thow partest nat so ligútly , by Seynt Ioún
Thow spake rigút now , of thilke traytour detú
That in this contre , alle oure frendes sletú
Haue heere my trouthe , as thow art his espie
Telle where he is , or thow shalt it a bye
By god , and by the holy sacrement
For sotúly thow art , on of his assent
To slen vs ©onge folk , thow false thef
Now sires q¶ he , if that ©ow be so lef
To fynde detú , turne vp , this croked wey
For in that Groue , I lefte hi~ be my fey
Vnder a tree , and there he wol a byde
Nat for ©oure bost , he wol hiö no thyngö hyde
Se ©e that Oke , rigút there ©e shuln hi~ fynde
God saue ©ow , that bougút a geyn man kynde
And ©ow amende , thus seide this olde man
And euäicú of theise riotours , ran
Til he come to that tree , and there fey found
Of floreyns fyn , of gold I koyned round
Wel ny an eigúte Busshels , as hem thougút
No lengere tha~ne , after detú they sougút
But ecú of hem , so glad was of the sigút
For that the floreyns , ben so faire and brigút
